taxonomy: Conchograecum oliveirae is classified as a species within the plant family Bignoniaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the bignonia or bottlebrush family. Bignoniaceae is a family of around 130 genera and over 1,000 species, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The family is known for its diverse range of plants, including vines, shrubs, and trees. These plants often have showy flowers and are popular in horticulture for their ornamental value.
